title: Discounted Payback Period: If a project has a cost of 80000 and annual...
summary: Calculate the discounted payback period by accumulating discounted cash flows until the investment is recovered.
prompt: If a project has a cost of $80,000 and annual cash flows of $25,000 for 4 years, what is the discounted payback period at a discount rate of 10%?

options.text: Payback ignores time value of money
Discount each cash flow and find the payback point

solution: Discount each cash flow and find the payback point. The calculation shows that it takes about 3.5 years to recover the cost.
